lede: A look at August 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rajiv Gandhi and Ron Paul, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Spanair Flight 5022
events.desc: Spanair Flight 5022, from Madrid, Spain to Gran Canaria, skids off the runway and crashes at Barajas Airport. Of the 172 people on board, 146 die immediately, and eight more later die of injuries sustained in the crash.

events.title: Estonia
events.desc: Estonia, occupied by and incorporated into the Soviet Union in 1940, issues a decision on the re-establishment of independence on the basis of legal continuity of its pre-occupation statehood.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Eight British soldiers are killed and 28 wounded when their bus is hit by an IRA roadside bomb in Ballygawley, County Tyrone.

events.title: Hungary
events.desc: Hungary adopts the Hungarian Constitution of 1949 and becomes a People's Republic.

events.title: New York City
events.desc: Soviet Consul General in New York, Jacob M. Lomakin is expelled by the United States, due to the Kasenkina Case.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: British Prime Minister Winston Churchill makes the fourth of his famous wartime speeches, containing the line "Never was so much owed by so many to so few".

events.title: National Football League
events.desc: The National Football League is organized as the American Professional Football Conference in Canton, Ohio

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Brussels is captured during the German invasion of Belgium.

events.title: War of the Spanish Succession
events.desc: War of the Spanish Succession: A multinational army led by the Austrian commander Guido Starhemberg defeats the Spanish-Bourbon army commanded by Alexandre Maître, Marquis de Bay in the Battle of Saragossa.

events.title: Siege of Pensacola (1707)
events.desc: The first Siege of Pensacola comes to an end with the failure of the British to capture Pensacola, Florida.

births.name: Rajiv Gandhi
births.line: Indian lawyer and politician, 6th Prime Minister of India

births.name: Bernardo O'Higgins
births.line: Chilean general and politician, 2nd Supreme Director of Chile

births.name: Benjamin Harrison
births.line: American general, lawyer, and politician, 23rd President of the United States

deaths.text: Hua Guofeng, Chinese politician, 2nd Premier of the People's Republic of China
Pope Pius X, Head of the Catholic Church from 1903 to 1914
Georg von Frundsberg, German knight and landowner
AD 14, Roman son of Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa (born 12 BC)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Heliodorus of Bet Zabdai
